science.title: Scientists Reverse Stroke Damage Using Stem Cells in Breakthrough Study
science.summary: Scientists have discovered that transplanted stem cell-derived brain cells may do far more than simply survive after a stroke. science.title: Earth is flying through ancient supernova debris and scientists found the evidence in Antarctic ice
science.summary: Earth is quietly collecting radioactive debris from an ancient stellar explosion as our Solar System drifts through a giant cloud of gas and dust between the stars. science.title: Scientists make old blood stem cells young again in major anti-aging breakthrough
science.summary: As blood stem cells age, their lysosomes become overactive and damaged, triggering inflammation and weakening the body’s ability to regenerate healthy blood and immune cells. science.title: Paleontology rocked by discovery of organic molecules in 66-million-year-old dinosaur bones
science.summary: Scientists have uncovered compelling evidence that dinosaur fossils may still contain traces of their original proteins, overturning a long-standing belief that fossilization destroys all organic material. science.title: Who are the Japanese? Huge DNA discovery rewrites history
science.summary: Scientists analyzing the genomes of thousands of people across Japan discovered evidence for a previously overlooked third ancestral group, challenging the long-accepted “dual origins” theory. tech.title: Desperate Trump taps "Tim Apple," Jensen Huang, Elon Musk to attend Xi summit
tech.summary: Xi meeting may force Trump to pivot on chip restrictions and Taiwan.

tech.title: OpenAI says hackers stole some data after latest code security issue
tech.summary: OpenAI said the damage was limited to the employees’ devices and did not affect user data nor its production systems, and none of its intellectual property was stolen.

tech.title: Spotify to adopt Apple’s new video podcast tech, offering creators easier cross-platform distribution
tech.summary: Spotify will let creators distribute and monetize video podcasts on Apple Podcasts using Apple’s HLS streaming technology, without changing their existing workflows.

tech.title: Linux devs are fighting the new age-gated internet
tech.summary: In January, Colorado lawmakers introduced a proposal to make operating systems collect users' ages and pass them to app developers. tech.title: An Engineer’s Post Protesting Laptop Surveillance Is Going Viral Inside Meta
tech.summary: Meta employees in the US and UK are organizing against corporate software that tracks workers’ keystrokes and mouse activity.

medicine.title: A common constipation drug shows surprising power to protect kidneys
medicine.summary: A common constipation drug may have unexpectedly unlocked a new way to slow chronic kidney disease — a condition that affects millions and often leads to dialysis. medicine.title: Experimental drug shrinks advanced kidney tumors, clears cancer in one patient
medicine.summary: A new Phase I clinical trial provides proof of concept for a potential therapy for treatment-resistant cancers, particularly kidney cancer. In the new study, published in Cell Reports Medicine, researchers tested a novel kind of immunotherapy in individuals with different types of cancer.

medicine.title: Colon cancer breakthrough keeps patients cancer-free for nearly 3 years
medicine.summary: A short burst of immunotherapy before surgery is delivering surprisingly powerful results for a specific type of colorectal cancer. medicine.title: Breakthrough biomaterial heals tissue from the inside out
medicine.summary: Scientists have developed a breakthrough injectable biomaterial that travels through the bloodstream to repair damaged tissue from within, reducing inflammation and jumpstarting healing. mathematics.title: In Math, Rigor Is Vital. But Are Digitized Proofs Taking It Too Far?
mathematics.summary: The quest to make mathematics rigorous has a long and spotty history — one mathematicians can learn from as they push to formalize everything in the computer program Lean. mathematics.title: MIT affiliates win AI for Math grants to accelerate mathematical discovery
mathematics.summary: Department of Mathematics researchers David Roe and Andrew Sutherland seek to advance automated theorem proving; four additional MIT alumni also awarded.

mathematics.title: School of Science welcomed new faculty in 2024
mathematics.summary: Eleven new professors join the departments of Biology; Brain and Cognitive Sciences; Earth, Atmospheric and Planetary Sciences; Mathematics; and Physics.

mathematics.title: Laurent Demanet appointed co-director of MIT Center for Computational Science and Engineering
mathematics.summary: Applied mathematics professor will join fellow co-director Nicolas Hadjiconstantinou in leading the cross-cutting center.

space.title: NASA Outlines Preliminary Artemis III Mission Plans

space.title: SpaceX's Starship V3 megarocket finally has a debut launch date. Here's when it will fly
space.summary: SpaceX is officially targeting May 19 for the debut of Starship Version 3, an advanced new vehicle that could help humanity take its first steps on the moon and Mars.

space.title: SpaceX's Starship V3 megarocket will do something completely new on Flight 12 — take a good look at itself
space.summary: SpaceX's advanced new "V3" Starship megarocket is set to fly for the first time ever next week. The debut will feature some in-space action never seen before on a Starship mission.

astronomy.title: We've Been Listening for Ten Years. Here's What We Heard
astronomy.summary: For ten years, astronomers at UCLA have been pointing one of the world's most powerful radio telescopes at the stars and listening. astronomy.summary: One of the core community surveys of NASA’s Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, the Galactic Bulge Time-Domain Survey, is expected to locate over a thousand exoplanets that orbit far away from their stars, beyond the orbital distance of Earth from the Sun. astronomy.title: We've Been Wasting 99% of Our Supernova Data
astronomy.summary: Every time an astronomer points a telescope at a distant supernova, they're trying to measure how far away it is. But the light from these stellar explosions arrives tangled up with interference from dust, the age of the host galaxy and the chemical make up of the original star . astronomy.title: Moon and Planets to Gather in Twilight Spectacle on May 18–20
astronomy.summary: Watch the crescent Moon dance with the planets when it returns next week. The post Moon and Planets to Gather in Twilight Spectacle on May 18–20 appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: A Black Hole’s Puzzling X-Ray Bursts
astronomy.summary: In 2019, a supermassive black hole in a galaxy 300 million light-years away woke up. Now, it’s puzzling astronomers with an unexpected slowdown in its X-ray bursts. The post A Black Hole’s Puzzling X-Ray Bursts appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

architecture.title: WUF13 in Baku and Stefano Boeri’s Ambrosian Monastery in Milan: This Week’s Review

architecture.title: The Productive Clash: Heritage Interiors, Contemporary Projects, and the Value of Imperfection
architecture.summary: Heritage, in interiors, is increasingly rarer to be only a matter of preservation alone. More often it arrives as friction: the encounter between what a building already is—its plan logic, its scars, its structural inconsistencies—and what contemporary life demands of it.

construction.title: Zachry Construction breaks ground on $108M Dallas water project
construction.summary: The infrastructure builder has been bullish on water projects in the Lone Star State and aims to finish work in 2029.

ai.title: Beyond Cooperative Simulators: Generating Realistic User Personas for Robust Evaluation of LLM Agents
ai.summary: arXiv:2605.12894v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Model (LLM) agents are increasingly deployed in settings where they interact with a wide variety of people, including users who are unclear, impatient, or reluctant to share information.
